#libs
```{r}
# Load required libraries
library(phyloseq)
library(microbiome)
library(vegan)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(forcats)
library(gridExtra)
library(patchwork)
library(ggforce)
library(speedyseq)
```


# permanova test 2
```{r}
# UPDATED Preprocess phyloseq objects with proper tax_glom handling
preprocess_phyloseq <- function(ps_obj, name, tax_glom_rank = NULL, prevalence = 0.1, detection = 0.01) {
  message(paste("Preprocessing", name))
  
  # Apply compositional transformation FIRST
  message(paste("Applying compositional transformation to", name))
  ps_obj <- microbiome::transform(ps_obj, "compositional")
  
  # Apply taxonomic agglomeration if specified and if taxonomy table exists
  if (!is.null(tax_glom_rank) && !is.na(tax_glom_rank)) {
    if (!is.null(tax_table(ps_obj, errorIfNULL = FALSE))) {
      message(paste("Agglomerating", name, "by", tax_glom_rank))
      ps_obj <- speedyseq::tax_glom(ps_obj, taxrank = tax_glom_rank, NArm = TRUE)
    } else {
      message(paste("No taxonomy table found for", name, "- skipping agglomeration"))
    }
  } else {
    message(paste("No taxonomic agglomeration for", name))
  }
  
  # Handle NAs by replacing with zeros
  otu <- as(otu_table(ps_obj), "matrix")
  if (any(is.na(otu))) {
    message(paste("Found NAs in", name, "- cleaning data"))
    otu[is.na(otu)] <- 0
    otu_table(ps_obj) <- otu_table(otu, taxa_are_rows = taxa_are_rows(ps_obj))
  }
  
  # Apply prevalence filtering AFTER transformation
  message(paste("Applying prevalence filtering for", name, "- prevalence:", prevalence, "detection:", detection))
  taxas <- core_members(ps_obj, detection = detection, prevalence = prevalence)
  
  # Check if any taxa passed filtering
  if (length(taxas) == 0) {
    message(paste("WARNING: No taxa passed prevalence filtering for", name))
    message(paste("Total taxa before filtering:", ntaxa(ps_obj)))
    message("Using all taxa without filtering")
    # Skip filtering if no taxa pass
  } else {
    ps_obj <- prune_taxa(taxas, ps_obj)
    message(paste("After filtering,", name, "has", ntaxa(ps_obj), "taxa"))
  }
  
  return(ps_obj)
}

# UPDATED Helper function to run a single PERMANOVA for preprocessed phyloseq
run_single_permanova <- function(ps_obj, name, formula, method = "bray") {
  
  message(paste("Running PERMANOVA for", name))
  
  # Convert sample data to a data frame
  metadf <- as.data.frame(as.matrix(ps_obj@sam_data))
  
  # Calculate distance matrix
  dist <- phyloseq::distance(ps_obj, method = method)
  
  # Create formula from the string
  formula1 <- as.formula(paste("dist ~", formula))
  
  # Perform adonis analysis with the specified formula
  permanova_result <- adonis2(formula1, data = metadf,by='margin', parallel = 30)
  
  # Process adonis results
  res <- as.data.frame(permanova_result)
  res$meta <- row.names(res)
  res <- subset(res, res$meta != 'Total')

  # Add significance markers
  res[which(res$`Pr(>F)` > 0.1), 'signif'] <- ' '
  res[which(res$`Pr(>F)` <=  0.1), 'signif'] <- '.'
  res[which(res$`Pr(>F)` <= 0.05), 'signif'] <- '*'
  res[which(res$`Pr(>F)` <= 0.01), 'signif'] <- '**'
  res[which(res$`Pr(>F)` <= 0.001), 'signif'] <- '***'
  
  # Keep original R2 values but also add percentage column for reference
  res$R2_percent <- res$R2 * 100
  
  # Add dataset name
  res$Dataset <- name
  
  # Print debugging info to help identify residuals row names
  message(paste("Row names in result for", name, ":", paste(res$meta, collapse=", ")))
  
  return(list(
    result = list(
      permanova_result = permanova_result,
      processed_result = res
    ),
    data = res
  ))
}

# CORRECTED Helper function to create the PERMANOVA plot with original significance symbols
create_permanova_plot <- function(data, title, color_palette, 
                                 x_label = "R²", 
                                 y_label = "Features") {
  
  # Add alpha based on significance: significant (p ≤ 0.1) gets alpha = 1, non-significant gets alpha = 0.5
  data$alpha_value <- ifelse(data$signif == ' ', 0.5, 1.0)
  
  # Create the grouped barplot with R2 values (not percentages)
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = R2, y = Feature, fill = Dataset, alpha = alpha_value)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.8) +
    scale_alpha_identity() +  # Use the alpha values directly
    
    # ADD R2 VALUES ON BARS - positioned inside the bars
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", R2)), 
              position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
              hjust = -0.1, size = 4, color = "black", fontface = "bold") +
    
    # SIGNIFICANCE STARS - ORIGINAL VERSION that works
    geom_text(aes(label = signif), 
              position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
              hjust=1.25, vjust=0, size = 10
              , color = "black", fontface = "bold") +
    
    labs(title = title,
         x = x_label,
         y = y_label) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_palette) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
          panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),
          axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
          axis.title.x = element_text( size = 17),    # X-axis title (R²)
          axis.title.y = element_text( size = 17),
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
          axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold",size=15, colour= 'black'),
          axis.text.x = element_text(size=15, colour= 'black'),
          # LEGEND 2x BIGGER
          legend.title = element_text(face = "bold",size=30,colour= 'black'),
          legend.text = element_text(size=25,colour= 'black'),
          legend.position = "right") +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.3)))  # Increased space for R2 values and stars
  
  return(p)
}

# Helper function to process combined data for plotting
process_combined_data <- function(combined_df) {
  if (nrow(combined_df) == 0) return(combined_df)
  
  # Calculate mean R2 per feature for ordering
  feature_means <- combined_df %>%
    group_by(meta) %>%
    summarize(mean_R2 = mean(R2, na.rm = TRUE))
  
  # Create ordered factor for features
  feature_order <- feature_means$meta[order(feature_means$mean_R2)]
  combined_df$Feature <- factor(combined_df$meta, levels = feature_order)
  
  return(combined_df)
}

# UPDATED Fixed create_final_improved_donut_plot function with smaller fonts and larger plot
create_final_improved_donut_plot <- function(variance_data, title = "Variance Explained", 
                                           color_palette = NULL, names_list = NULL) {
  library(ggplot2)
  library(ggforce)  # For geom_arc_bar
  
  # Transform data for pie charts
  pie_data <- data.frame()
  
  # FIX: If names_list is provided, use it to order the data and colors
  if (!is.null(names_list)) {
    # Ensure variance_data is ordered according to names_list
    variance_data$Dataset <- factor(variance_data$Dataset, levels = names_list)
    variance_data <- variance_data[order(variance_data$Dataset), ]
    
    # Create color mapping based on names_list order
    color_mapping <- setNames(color_palette[1:length(names_list)], names_list)
  } else {
    # Fallback to alphabetical order if names_list not provided
    unique_datasets <- unique(variance_data$Dataset)
    color_mapping <- setNames(color_palette[1:length(unique_datasets)], unique_datasets)
  }
  
  # Assign colors based on dataset names
  explained_colors <- character(nrow(variance_data))
  unexplained_colors <- character(nrow(variance_data))
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(variance_data)) {
    dataset_name <- as.character(variance_data$Dataset[i])
    if (dataset_name %in% names(color_mapping)) {
      explained_colors[i] <- color_mapping[dataset_name]
      unexplained_colors[i] <- color_mapping[dataset_name]
    } else {
      # Fallback for unknown dataset names
      explained_colors[i] <- color_palette[min(i, length(color_palette))]
      unexplained_colors[i] <- color_palette[min(i, length(color_palette))]
    }
  }
  
  # FIX: Create radius mapping based on names_list order if provided
  if (!is.null(names_list)) {
    # MAKE DONUT PLOT BIGGER by increasing radius values
    radius_mapping <- setNames(c(4, 3, 2), names_list)  # Increased from 3,2,1 to 4,3,2
  } else {
    # Default mapping with increased sizes
    radius_mapping <- c("Species" = 4, "Pathways" = 3, "MAGs" = 2)  # Increased sizes
  }
  
  radius_order <- numeric(nrow(variance_data))
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(variance_data)) {
    dataset_name <- as.character(variance_data$Dataset[i])
    if (dataset_name %in% names(radius_mapping)) {
      radius_order[i] <- radius_mapping[dataset_name]
    } else {
      # Default ordering for unknown datasets
      radius_order[i] <- nrow(variance_data) - i + 1
    }
  }
  
  # For each dataset, create a row for each segment
  for (i in 1:nrow(variance_data)) {
    dataset <- as.character(variance_data$Dataset[i])
    radius <- radius_order[i]  # Use the assigned radius
    
    # Add Explained segment
    pie_data <- rbind(pie_data, data.frame(
      dataset = dataset,
      type = "Explained",
      value = variance_data$Explained[i],
      percentage = variance_data$Explained[i] * 100,
      radius = radius,
      fill_color = explained_colors[i],
      alpha_value = 1.0,  # Full opacity for explained
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
    
    # Add Unexplained segment
    pie_data <- rbind(pie_data, data.frame(
      dataset = dataset,
      type = "Unexplained",
      value = variance_data$Unexplained[i],
      percentage = variance_data$Unexplained[i] * 100,
      radius = radius,
      fill_color = unexplained_colors[i],
      alpha_value = 0.5,  # Semi-transparent for unexplained
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
  }
  
  # Sort data by dataset and segment type
  pie_data <- pie_data[order(pie_data$radius, pie_data$type), ]
  
  # Calculate start and end angles for each segment
  # Process each dataset separately to get cumulative fractions
  for (ds in unique(pie_data$dataset)) {
    ds_rows <- which(pie_data$dataset == ds)
    
    # Get total for this dataset (should be 1.0)
    ds_total <- sum(pie_data$value[ds_rows])
    
    # Calculate fractions
    pie_data$fraction[ds_rows] <- pie_data$value[ds_rows] / ds_total
    
    # Calculate end angles (cumulative)
    end_angle <- cumsum(pie_data$fraction[ds_rows]) * 2 * pi
    
    # Calculate start angles
    start_angle <- c(0, end_angle[-length(end_angle)])
    
    # Assign to data frame
    pie_data$start[ds_rows] <- start_angle
    pie_data$end[ds_rows] <- end_angle
    
    # Calculate midpoint for label placement
    pie_data$mid[ds_rows] <- (start_angle + end_angle) / 2
  }
  
  # Create the base plot
  p <- ggplot(pie_data) +
    geom_arc_bar(aes(
      x0 = 0, y0 = 0, 
      r0 = radius - 0.4, r = radius + 0.4,
      start = start, end = end, 
      fill = fill_color,
      alpha = alpha_value  # Add alpha aesthetic
    )) +
    coord_fixed() +
    theme_void() +
    labs(title = title)
  
  # Manually set colors with alpha
  fill_values <- c(explained_colors[1:nrow(variance_data)], unexplained_colors[1:nrow(variance_data)])
  names(fill_values) <- c(explained_colors[1:nrow(variance_data)], unexplained_colors[1:nrow(variance_data)])
  
  p <- p + scale_fill_identity() + scale_alpha_identity()  # Use identity scales for custom colors and alpha
  
  # Add percentage labels INSIDE the segments
  # Create segment_info based on actual dataset names and their positions
  segment_info <- list()
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(variance_data)) {
    dataset_name <- as.character(variance_data$Dataset[i])
    if (dataset_name %in% names(radius_mapping)) {
      r <- radius_mapping[dataset_name]
    } else {
      r <- nrow(variance_data) - i + 1  # Default ordering
    }
    
    # Add explained
    segment_info <- append(segment_info, list(list(
      dataset = dataset_name,
      type = "Explained",
      r = r,
      angle = pi/2 - pi/12,
      perc = round(variance_data$Explained[i] * 100)
    )))
    
    # Add unexplained
    segment_info <- append(segment_info, list(list(
      dataset = dataset_name,
      type = "Unexplained",
      r = r,
      angle = pi/2 + pi/12,
      perc = round(variance_data$Unexplained[i] * 100)
    )))
  }
  
  # Add percentage labels with manual positioning - SMALLER FONT (changed from 5 to 3)
  for (seg in segment_info) {
    p <- p + annotate(
      "text",
      x = seg$r * cos(seg$angle),
      y = seg$r * sin(seg$angle),
      label = paste0(seg$perc, "%"),
      color = "black",
      fontface = "bold",
      size = 3  # SMALLER FONT (was 5)
    )
  }
  
  # Position category headers with more space - SMALLER FONT (changed from 5 to 4)
  p <- p + annotate("text", x = 8, y = 4, label = "Explained Variance", 
                    fontface = "bold", size = 4, hjust = 1)  # Smaller font
  p <- p + annotate("text", x = -8, y = 4, label = "Unexplained Variance", 
                    fontface = "bold", size = 4, hjust = 0)  # Smaller font
  
  # Get reference points for each segment using correct radius mapping
  reference_points <- list()
  
  # Build reference points based on actual order in names_list if provided
  if (!is.null(names_list)) {
    for (i in 1:length(names_list)) {
      dataset <- names_list[i]
      r <- radius_mapping[dataset]
      
      # RIGHT SIDE (Explained)
      reference_points <- append(reference_points, list(list(
        dataset = dataset, side = "right", 
        x = r * cos(pi/2 - pi/12), y = r * sin(pi/2 - pi/12)
      )))
      
      # LEFT SIDE (Unexplained)
      reference_points <- append(reference_points, list(list(
        dataset = dataset, side = "left", 
        x = r * cos(pi/2 + pi/12), y = r * sin(pi/2 + pi/12)
      )))
    }
  } else {
    # Default reference points if names_list not provided
    reference_points <- list(
      # RIGHT SIDE (Explained)
      list(dataset = "Species", side = "right", x = 4 * cos(pi/2 - pi/12), y = 4 * sin(pi/2 - pi/12)),
      list(dataset = "Pathways", side = "right", x = 3 * cos(pi/2 - pi/12), y = 3 * sin(pi/2 - pi/12)),
      list(dataset = "MAGs", side = "right", x = 2 * cos(pi/2 - pi/12), y = 2 * sin(pi/2 - pi/12)),
      
      # LEFT SIDE (Unexplained)
      list(dataset = "Species", side = "left", x = 4 * cos(pi/2 + pi/12), y = 4 * sin(pi/2 + pi/12)),
      list(dataset = "Pathways", side = "left", x = 3 * cos(pi/2 + pi/12), y = 3 * sin(pi/2 + pi/12)),
      list(dataset = "MAGs", side = "left", x = 2 * cos(pi/2 + pi/12), y = 2 * sin(pi/2 + pi/12))
    )
  }
  
  # Update the label colors to match the new alpha system
  # For explained variance (full color) - using color_mapping
  explained_label_colors <- character(nrow(variance_data))
  # For unexplained variance (lighter appearance due to alpha - create lighter versions)
  unexplained_label_colors <- character(nrow(variance_data))
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(variance_data)) {
    dataset_name <- as.character(variance_data$Dataset[i])
    if (dataset_name %in% names(color_mapping)) {
      explained_label_colors[i] <- color_mapping[dataset_name]
      unexplained_label_colors[i] <- adjustcolor(color_mapping[dataset_name], alpha.f = 0.7)
    } else {
      explained_label_colors[i] <- color_palette[min(i, length(color_palette))]
      unexplained_label_colors[i] <- adjustcolor(color_palette[min(i, length(color_palette))], alpha.f = 0.7)
    }
  }
  
  # Define dataset display order from names_list or default
  dataset_order <- if (!is.null(names_list)) names_list else c("Species", "Pathways", "MAGs")
  
  # Add RIGHT side labels with connections to Explained segments - SMALLER FONT (changed from 4.5 to 3.5)
  for (i in 1:length(dataset_order)) {
    dataset <- dataset_order[i]
    dataset_index <- which(as.character(variance_data$Dataset) == dataset)
    
    if (length(dataset_index) > 0) {
      # Find the correct reference point based on the dataset
      ref_idx <- which(sapply(reference_points, function(x) x$dataset == dataset && x$side == "right"))
      
      if (length(ref_idx) > 0) {
        ref_point <- reference_points[[ref_idx[1]]]
        
        # Add dataset label with MORE SPACE
        p <- p + annotate(
          "text",
          x = 8.5,  # Increased distance from end of line
          y = 2.5 - (i-1) * 0.7,
          label = dataset,
          color = explained_label_colors[dataset_index],  # Use explained colors
          fontface = "bold",
          size = 3.5,  # SMALLER FONT (was 4.5)
          hjust = 1
        )
        
        # Add connection line - STOPPING FARTHER FROM TEXT
        p <- p + annotate(
          "segment",
          x = ref_point$x,
          y = ref_point$y,
          xend = 7.5,  # Stop the line with MORE gap before text
          yend = 2.5 - (i-1) * 0.7,
          color = "black",
          size = 0.2
        )
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Add LEFT side labels with connections to Unexplained segments - SMALLER FONT (changed from 4.5 to 3.5)
  for (i in 1:length(dataset_order)) {
    dataset <- dataset_order[i]
    dataset_index <- which(as.character(variance_data$Dataset) == dataset)
    
    if (length(dataset_index) > 0) {
      # Find the correct reference point based on the dataset
      ref_idx <- which(sapply(reference_points, function(x) x$dataset == dataset && x$side == "left"))
      
      if (length(ref_idx) > 0) {
        ref_point <- reference_points[[ref_idx[1]]]
        
        # Add dataset label with MORE SPACE
        p <- p + annotate(
          "text",
          x = -8.5,  # Increased distance from end of line
          y = 2.5 - (i-1) * 0.7,
          label = dataset,
          color = unexplained_label_colors[dataset_index],  # Use lighter colors
          fontface = "bold",
          size = 3.5,  # SMALLER FONT (was 4.5)
          hjust = 0
        )
        
        # Add connection line - STOPPING FARTHER FROM TEXT
        p <- p + annotate(
          "segment",
          x = ref_point$x,
          y = ref_point$y,
          xend = -7.5,  # Stop the line with MORE gap before text
          yend = 2.5 - (i-1) * 0.7,
          color = "black",
          size = 0.2
        )
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Finalize plot with EXPANDED LIMITS for larger donut
  p <- p + 
    xlim(-10, 10) +  # Expanded limits to accommodate larger donut
    ylim(-5, 5) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "none"  # Remove legend since colors are clear from the alpha
    )
  
  return(p)
}

# UPDATED Function for running custom PERMANOVA models with separate preprocessing
run_custom_permanova_plots <- function(ps_list, names_list, formula_list, 
                                     title_prefix = "PERMANOVA Results",
                                     color_palette = NULL,
                                     tax_glom_list = NULL,
                                     prevalence = 0.1,
                                     detection = 0.01) {
  
  # Required libraries
  require(phyloseq)
  require(microbiome)
  require(vegan)
  require(ggplot2)
  require(dplyr)
  require(tidyr)
  require(forcats)
  require(gridExtra)
  require(patchwork) # For combining plots
  
  # Check inputs
  if (!is.list(ps_list)) {
    stop("ps_list must be a list of phyloseq objects")
  }
  if (length(ps_list) != length(names_list)) {
    stop("ps_list and names_list must have the same length")
  }
  if (length(ps_list) != length(formula_list)) {
    stop("ps_list and formula_list must have the same length")
  }
  
  # Set default taxonomic agglomeration if not provided
  if (is.null(tax_glom_list)) {
    tax_glom_list <- rep(NA, length(ps_list))
  }
  
  # Set default color palette if not provided
  if (is.null(color_palette)) {
    color_palette <- c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73", "#F0E442", "#0072B2", "#D55E00", "#CC79A7", "#999999")
    # Extend color palette if needed
    if (length(ps_list) > length(color_palette)) {
      color_palette <- colorRampPalette(color_palette)(length(ps_list))
    }
  }
  
  # IMPORTANT: Create a named color vector that matches names_list order
  named_colors <- setNames(color_palette[1:length(names_list)], names_list)
  
  # Create empty lists to store results
  bray_results <- list(all_results = list(), combined_data = data.frame())
  
  # Create data frames to store variance values for each dataset
  bray_variance <- data.frame(Dataset = character(), Explained = numeric(), Unexplained = numeric(), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  
  # PREPROCESS ALL DATASETS FIRST
  preprocessed_ps_list <- list()
  for (i in 1:length(ps_list)) {
    ps_obj <- ps_list[[i]]
    name <- names_list[i]
    tax_glom_rank <- tax_glom_list[[i]]
    
    preprocessed_ps <- preprocess_phyloseq(
      ps_obj = ps_obj,
      name = name,
      tax_glom_rank = tax_glom_rank,
      prevalence = prevalence,
      detection = detection
    )
    preprocessed_ps_list[[i]] <- preprocessed_ps
  }
  
  # THEN RUN PERMANOVA ON PREPROCESSED DATA
  for (i in 1:length(preprocessed_ps_list)) {
    ps_obj <- preprocessed_ps_list[[i]]
    name <- names_list[i]
    formula <- formula_list[[i]]
    
    # Try to run Bray-Curtis analysis
    tryCatch({
      bray_result <- run_single_permanova(
        ps_obj = ps_obj,
        name = name,
        formula = formula,
        method = "bray"
      )
      
      # Check if we got valid results
      if (!is.null(bray_result$result$permanova_result) && nrow(bray_result$data) > 0) {
        # Store full results
        bray_results$all_results[[name]] <- bray_result$result
        
        # Add to combined data frame (for full results reference)
        bray_results$combined_data <- rbind(bray_results$combined_data, bray_result$data)
        
        # Get the residuals row by checking for both possible row names
        residuals_row <- bray_result$data %>% 
          filter(grepl("^Residual", meta, ignore.case = TRUE) | meta == "other")
        
        # Calculate explained variance as 1 - R2 of residual
        # Default to 0 if no residual row found (should not happen in normal adonis2 output)
        explained_variance <- if(nrow(residuals_row) > 0) {
          1 - residuals_row$R2[1]  # Using raw R2, not percentage
        } else {
          message(paste("Warning: No residual row found for", name, "with Bray-Curtis method"))
          0
        }
        
        # Calculate unexplained variance
        unexplained_variance <- 1 - explained_variance
        
        # Store the variance values for this dataset
        bray_variance <- rbind(bray_variance, data.frame(
          Dataset = name,
          Explained = explained_variance,
          Unexplained = unexplained_variance,
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        ))
      } else {
        message(paste("No valid results for", name, "- skipping"))
      }
      
    }, error = function(e) {
      message(paste("Error in Bray-Curtis analysis for", name, ":", e$message))
    })
  }
  
  # Process combined data for plotting (if we have results)
  bray_plot <- NULL
  bray_combined_plot <- NULL
  if (nrow(bray_results$combined_data) > 0) {
    # Improved residuals removal with case-insensitive pattern matching
    bray_plot_data <- bray_results$combined_data %>% 
      filter(!grepl("^Residual", meta, ignore.case = TRUE) & meta != "other")
    
    # FIX: Ensure Dataset column is ordered according to names_list
    bray_plot_data$Dataset <- factor(bray_plot_data$Dataset, levels = names_list)
    
    # Process the filtered data
    bray_plot_data <- process_combined_data(bray_plot_data)
    
    # Create the plot without residuals
    bray_plot <- create_permanova_plot(
      data = bray_plot_data,
      title = paste0(title_prefix, ": Bray-Curtis (Compositional)"),
      color_palette = named_colors  # Use named colors
    )
    
    # Create COMBINED bar + donut plot for Bray-Curtis
    if (nrow(bray_variance) > 0) {
      # FIX: Ensure variance data is also ordered according to names_list
      bray_variance$Dataset <- factor(bray_variance$Dataset, levels = names_list)
      bray_variance <- bray_variance[order(bray_variance$Dataset), ]
      
      bray_donut_plot <- create_final_improved_donut_plot(
        variance_data = bray_variance,
        title = "Variance Explained (Bray-Curtis)",
        color_palette = color_palette,  # Pass the original palette
        names_list = names_list  # Pass names_list for ordering
      )
      
      # Place bar plot and donut plot side by side
      bray_combined_plot <- bray_plot + bray_donut_plot + plot_layout(ncol = 2, widths = c(2, 2))
    }
  } else {
    message("No Bray-Curtis results available for plotting")
  }
  
  # Create donut plot
  combined_donut_plot <- NULL
  if (nrow(bray_variance) > 0) {
    combined_donut_plot <- create_final_improved_donut_plot(
      variance_data = bray_variance,
      title = "Variance Explained (Bray-Curtis)",
      color_palette = color_palette,
      names_list = names_list
    )
  }
  
  # Return results
  return(list(
    bray_results = bray_results,
    bray_plot = bray_plot,
    bray_combined_plot = bray_combined_plot,
    combined_donut_plot = combined_donut_plot,
    bray_variance = bray_variance,
    preprocessed_ps_list = preprocessed_ps_list  # Return preprocessed data for inspection
  ))
}
```
#run PERMANOVA
```{r}
# Load your phyloseq objects with updated paths
hm_phyloseq_joint <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/hm3/RSF_ps_humann_joint.rds')
mags_ps_filtered <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_ps_mags.rds')
ps_wgs <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v2.rds')

ps_list <- list(ps_wgs, hm_phyloseq_joint, mags_ps_filtered)
#names_list <- c("Species", "Pathways", "MAGs")
names_list <- c("Species", "Pathways", "MAGs")

# DEFINE YOUR SPECIFIC FORMULAS FOR EACH DATASET
formula_pathways <- '(Sex + Hospital + Department + Current_Smoking + Antibiotics_Last_Year + Antihypertensive_Drugs + Anticoagulants + Beta_blockers + Proton_Pump_Inhibitors + Corticosteroids + Antineoplastic_Drugs + Antidepressants + Antilipidemic_Drugs + Hypoglycemic_Drugs + Asthma_Treatment_Drugs + NSAIDs + Antiarrhythmic_Drugs + Antibiotics_used + Age_group + Timepoint)*Group'

formula_Species <- '(Sex + Hospital + Current_Smoking + Antibiotics_Last_Year + Beta_blockers + Proton_Pump_Inhibitors + Corticosteroids + Antidepressants+ Antibiotics_used + Age_group + BMI_group + Timepoint)*Group'

formula_mags <- '(Sex + Hospital + Department + Current_Smoking + Antibiotics_Last_Year + Antihypertensive_Drugs + Anticoagulants + Beta_blockers + Proton_Pump_Inhibitors + Antineoplastic_Drugs + Hypoglycemic_Drugs + Asthma_Treatment_Drugs + NSAIDs + Antiarrhythmic_Drugs + Antihistamines + Antibiotics_used + Age_group + BMI_group + Timepoint)*Group'

formula_list <- list(formula_Species, formula_pathways, formula_mags)

# FIXED: DEFINE TAXONOMIC AGGLOMERATION FOR EACH DATASET
# WGS: agglomerate by Species
# Pathways: no agglomeration (set to NA)
# MAGs: no agglomeration (set to NA)
#tax_glom_list <- list("Species", NA, "Species")
tax_glom_list <- list("Species", NA, "Species")

# Run the function with your specific models, filtering, and color palette
custom_results <- run_custom_permanova_plots(
  ps_list = ps_list,
  names_list = names_list,
  formula_list = formula_list,
  title_prefix = "Impact on Microbial Community",
  color_palette = c("#009E73", "#E69F00", "#56B4E9"),  # Green, Orange, Blue
  tax_glom_list = tax_glom_list,
  prevalence = 10/100,    # 10% prevalence
  detection = 1/100     # 1% detection
)

# Display different plot combinations:

# Option 1: Show only donut plot
print(custom_results$combined_donut_plot)

# Option 2: Show bar chart + donut plot combined
bray_combined_plot <- custom_results$bray_combined_plot
print(bray_combined_plot)

# Option 3: Show only bar chart
print(custom_results$bray_plot)

# Save plots in both PNG and PDF formats
# Combined plot (bar + donut)
ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_bray_custom.png', dpi=300, width=24, height=12, bray_combined_plot)
ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_bray_custom.pdf', width=24, height=12, bray_combined_plot)

# Individual bar plot
ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_bar_plot.png', dpi=300, width=35, height=25, custom_results$bray_plot)
ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_bar_plot.pdf', width=35, height=25, custom_results$bray_plot)

# Individual donut plot
ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_donut_plot.png', dpi=300, width=15, height=12, custom_results$combined_donut_plot)
ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_donut_plot.pdf', width=15, height=12, custom_results$combined_donut_plot)

# Save individual component plots as well
if (!is.null(custom_results$bray_plot)) {
  ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_bar_only.png', dpi=300, width=20, height=12, custom_results$bray_plot)
  ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_bar_only.pdf', width=20, height=12, custom_results$bray_plot)
}

if (!is.null(custom_results$combined_donut_plot)) {
  ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_donut_only.png', dpi=300, width=12, height=10, custom_results$combined_donut_plot)
  ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_donut_only.pdf', width=12, height=10, custom_results$combined_donut_plot)
}

# Print confirmation message
message("Plots saved successfully!")
message("PNG files saved with 300 DPI for high resolution")
message("PDF files saved for publication quality vector graphics")

# You can also access the raw results if needed
print(custom_results$bray_results)
print(custom_results$bray_variance)
```


# permanova r2 ajd FIXED
```{r}
# permanova test 2 - MODIFIED TO USE ADJUSTED R2, CORRECT SEQUENCE, ZERO-SUM REMOVAL

# CORRECTED Preprocess phyloseq objects with proper sequence and zero-sum removal
preprocess_phyloseq <- function(ps_obj, name, tax_glom_rank = NULL, prevalence = 0.1, detection = 0.01) {
  message(paste("Preprocessing", name))
  
  # STEP 1: Taxonomic agglomeration (if specified)
  if (!is.null(tax_glom_rank) && !is.na(tax_glom_rank)) {
    if (!is.null(tax_table(ps_obj, errorIfNULL = FALSE))) {
      message(paste("Agglomerating", name, "by", tax_glom_rank, "(STEP 1)"))
      ps_obj <- speedyseq::tax_glom(ps_obj, taxrank = tax_glom_rank, NArm = TRUE)
      message(paste("After agglomeration:", name, "has", ntaxa(ps_obj), "taxa"))
    } else {
      message(paste("No taxonomy table found for", name, "- skipping agglomeration"))
    }
  } else {
    message(paste("No taxonomic agglomeration for", name))
  }
  
  # Handle NAs by replacing with zeros BEFORE filtering
  otu <- as(otu_table(ps_obj), "matrix")
  if (any(is.na(otu))) {
    message(paste("Found NAs in", name, "- cleaning data"))
    otu[is.na(otu)] <- 0
    otu_table(ps_obj) <- otu_table(otu, taxa_are_rows = taxa_are_rows(ps_obj))
  }
  
  # STEP 2: Prevalence filtering (using relative abundances, but keep counts for later)
  message(paste("Applying prevalence filtering for", name, "(STEP 2) - prevalence:", prevalence, "detection:", detection))
  
  # Convert to relative for filtering, then back to counts
  ps_rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps_obj, function(x) x/sum(x))
  taxas <- core_members(ps_rel, detection = detection, prevalence = prevalence)
  
  if (length(taxas) == 0) {
    message(paste("WARNING: No taxa passed prevalence filtering for", name))
    message(paste("Total taxa before filtering:", ntaxa(ps_obj)))
    message("Using all taxa without filtering")
  } else {
    ps_obj <- prune_taxa(taxas, ps_obj)
    message(paste("After filtering:", name, "has", ntaxa(ps_obj), "taxa"))
  }
  
  # STEP 2b: Remove samples with zero total abundance after filtering
  sample_sums <- sample_sums(ps_obj)
  if (any(sample_sums == 0)) {
    zero_samples <- names(sample_sums[sample_sums == 0])
    message(paste("Removing", length(zero_samples), "samples with zero total abundance in", name))
    message("Zero samples: ", paste(zero_samples, collapse=", "))
    ps_obj <- prune_samples(sample_sums > 0, ps_obj)
    message(paste("After removing zero-sum samples:", name, "has", nsamples(ps_obj), "samples"))
  }
  
  # STEP 3: Compositional transformation (final step)
  message(paste("Applying compositional transformation to", name, "(STEP 3)"))
  ps_obj <- microbiome::transform(ps_obj, "compositional")
  
  return(ps_obj)
}

# Helper function to run a single PERMANOVA for preprocessed phyloseq (with adjusted R2)
run_single_permanova <- function(ps_obj, name, formula, method = "bray") {
  
  message(paste("Running PERMANOVA for", name))
  
  # Convert sample data to a data frame
  metadf <- as.data.frame(as.matrix(ps_obj@sam_data))
  
  # Optional: final check for zero-sum samples (should already be removed)
  sample_sums <- sample_sums(ps_obj)
  if (any(sample_sums == 0)) {
    zero_samples <- names(sample_sums[sample_sums == 0])
    message(paste("Warning: Removing", length(zero_samples), "samples with zero total abundance in", name))
    ps_obj <- prune_samples(sample_sums > 0, ps_obj)
    metadf <- metadf[rownames(metadf) %in% sample_names(ps_obj), , drop = FALSE]
  }
  
  # Calculate distance matrix
  dist <- phyloseq::distance(ps_obj, method = method)
  
  # Create formula from the string
  formula1 <- as.formula(paste("dist ~", formula))
  
  # Perform adonis analysis with the specified formula
  permanova_result <- adonis2(formula1, data = metadf,by='margin', parallel = 30) # use by='terms' if needed
  
  # Process adonis results
  res <- as.data.frame(permanova_result)
  res$meta <- row.names(res)
  res <- subset(res, res$meta != 'Total')
  
  # Calculate adjusted R2 values
  n_samples <- nrow(metadf)
  res$adjR2 <- NA
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(res)) {
    if (res$meta[i] != "Residual") {
      p_term <- res$Df[i]  # degrees of freedom for the term
      r2 <- res$R2[i]
      if (r2 < 1) {
        adj_r2 <- 1 - (1 - r2) * (n_samples - 1) / (n_samples - p_term - 1)
        res$adjR2[i] <- max(0, adj_r2)
      } else {
        res$adjR2[i] <- 1
      }
    }
  }
  res$adjR2[res$meta == "Residual"] <- NA
  
  # Add significance markers
  res[which(res$`Pr(>F)` > 0.1), 'signif'] <- ' '
  res[which(res$`Pr(>F)` <=  0.1), 'signif'] <- '.'
  res[which(res$`Pr(>F)` <= 0.05), 'signif'] <- '*'
  res[which(res$`Pr(>F)` <= 0.01), 'signif'] <- '**'
  res[which(res$`Pr(>F)` <= 0.001), 'signif'] <- '***'
  
  # Add percentages
  res$R2_percent <- res$R2 * 100
  res$adjR2_percent <- res$adjR2 * 100
  
  # Add dataset name
  res$Dataset <- name
  
  message(paste("Row names in result for", name, ":", paste(res$meta, collapse=", ")))
  
  return(list(
    result = list(
      permanova_result = permanova_result,
      processed_result = res
    ),
    data = res
  ))
}

# Helper function to create the PERMANOVA plot (uses adjusted or regular R2)
create_permanova_plot <- function(data, title, color_palette, 
                                 x_label = "Adjusted R²",
                                 y_label = "Features",
                                 use_adjusted_r2 = TRUE) {
  
  if (use_adjusted_r2) {
    data$plot_R2 <- data$adjR2
    message("Using ADJUSTED R² values for plotting")
  } else {
    data$plot_R2 <- data$R2
    message("Using regular R² values for plotting")
  }
  
  data$alpha_value <- ifelse(data$signif == ' ', 0.5, 1.0)
  
  p <- ggplot(data, aes(x = plot_R2, y = Feature, fill = Dataset, alpha = alpha_value)) +
    geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.8) +
    scale_alpha_identity() +
    geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.3f", plot_R2)), 
              position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
              hjust = -0.3, size = 5, color = "black", fontface = "bold") +
    geom_text(aes(label = signif), 
              position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), 
              hjust = 0.9, vjust = 0.5, size = 3, color = "black", fontface = "bold") +
    labs(title = title, x = x_label, y = y_label) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = color_palette) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
          panel.grid.minor.y = element_blank(),
          panel.border = element_rect(color = "black", fill = NA),
          axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
          axis.title.x = element_text(size = 17),
          axis.title.y = element_text(size = 17),
          plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
          axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15, colour = 'black'),
          axis.text.x = element_text(size = 15, colour = 'black'),
          legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 30, colour = 'black'),
          legend.text = element_text(size = 25, colour = 'black'),
          legend.position = "right") +
    scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.3)))
  
  return(p)
}

# Helper function to process combined data for ordering
process_combined_data <- function(combined_df, use_adjusted_r2 = TRUE) {
  if (nrow(combined_df) == 0) return(combined_df)
  
  if (use_adjusted_r2 && "adjR2" %in% colnames(combined_df)) {
    r2_col <- "adjR2"
    message("Using ADJUSTED R² for feature ordering")
  } else {
    r2_col <- "R2"
    message("Using regular R² for feature ordering")
  }
  
  feature_means <- combined_df %>%
    group_by(meta) %>%
    summarize(mean_R2 = mean(.data[[r2_col]], na.rm = TRUE))
  
  feature_order <- feature_means$meta[order(feature_means$mean_R2)]
  combined_df$Feature <- factor(combined_df$meta, levels = feature_order)
  
  return(combined_df)
}

# Updated donut plot function (adjusted R2 aware)
create_final_improved_donut_plot <- function(variance_data, title = "Variance Explained", 
                                           color_palette = NULL, names_list = NULL,
                                           use_adjusted_r2 = TRUE) {
  library(ggplot2)
  library(ggforce)
  
  if (use_adjusted_r2) {
    title <- "Adjusted Variance Explained"
    message("Using ADJUSTED R² for donut plot variance calculation")
  }
  
  pie_data <- data.frame()
  
  if (!is.null(names_list)) {
    variance_data$Dataset <- factor(variance_data$Dataset, levels = names_list)
    variance_data <- variance_data[order(variance_data$Dataset), ]
    color_mapping <- setNames(color_palette[1:length(names_list)], names_list)
  } else {
    unique_datasets <- unique(variance_data$Dataset)
    color_mapping <- setNames(color_palette[1:length(unique_datasets)], unique_datasets)
  }
  
  explained_colors <- character(nrow(variance_data))
  unexplained_colors <- character(nrow(variance_data))
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(variance_data)) {
    dataset_name <- as.character(variance_data$Dataset[i])
    if (dataset_name %in% names(color_mapping)) {
      explained_colors[i] <- color_mapping[dataset_name]
      unexplained_colors[i] <- color_mapping[dataset_name]
    } else {
      explained_colors[i] <- color_palette[min(i, length(color_palette))]
      unexplained_colors[i] <- color_palette[min(i, length(color_palette))]
    }
  }
  
  if (!is.null(names_list)) {
    radius_mapping <- setNames(c(4, 3, 2), names_list)
  } else {
    radius_mapping <- c("Species" = 4, "Pathways" = 3, "MAGs" = 2)
  }
  
  radius_order <- numeric(nrow(variance_data))
  for (i in 1:nrow(variance_data)) {
    dataset_name <- as.character(variance_data$Dataset[i])
    if (dataset_name %in% names(radius_mapping)) {
      radius_order[i] <- radius_mapping[dataset_name]
    } else {
      radius_order[i] <- nrow(variance_data) - i + 1
    }
  }
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(variance_data)) {
    dataset <- as.character(variance_data$Dataset[i])
    radius <- radius_order[i]
    
    pie_data <- rbind(pie_data, data.frame(
      dataset = dataset,
      type = "Explained",
      value = variance_data$Explained[i],
      percentage = variance_data$Explained[i] * 100,
      radius = radius,
      fill_color = explained_colors[i],
      alpha_value = 1.0,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
    
    pie_data <- rbind(pie_data, data.frame(
      dataset = dataset,
      type = "Unexplained",
      value = variance_data$Unexplained[i],
      percentage = variance_data$Unexplained[i] * 100,
      radius = radius,
      fill_color = unexplained_colors[i],
      alpha_value = 0.5,
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
  }
  
  pie_data <- pie_data[order(pie_data$radius, pie_data$type), ]
  
  for (ds in unique(pie_data$dataset)) {
    ds_rows <- which(pie_data$dataset == ds)
    ds_total <- sum(pie_data$value[ds_rows])
    pie_data$fraction[ds_rows] <- pie_data$value[ds_rows] / ds_total
    end_angle <- cumsum(pie_data$fraction[ds_rows]) * 2 * pi
    start_angle <- c(0, end_angle[-length(end_angle)])
    pie_data$start[ds_rows] <- start_angle
    pie_data$end[ds_rows] <- end_angle
    pie_data$mid[ds_rows] <- (start_angle + end_angle) / 2
  }
  
  p <- ggplot(pie_data) +
    geom_arc_bar(aes(x0 = 0, y0 = 0, r0 = radius - 0.4, r = radius + 0.4,
                     start = start, end = end, fill = fill_color, alpha = alpha_value)) +
    coord_fixed() +
    theme_void() +
    labs(title = title) +
    scale_fill_identity() + scale_alpha_identity()
  
  # Add percentage labels
  segment_info <- list()
  for (i in 1:nrow(variance_data)) {
    dataset_name <- as.character(variance_data$Dataset[i])
    if (dataset_name %in% names(radius_mapping)) {
      r <- radius_mapping[dataset_name]
    } else {
      r <- nrow(variance_data) - i + 1
    }
    segment_info <- append(segment_info, list(list(
      dataset = dataset_name, type = "Explained", r = r,
      angle = pi/2 - pi/12,
      perc = round(variance_data$Explained[i] * 100)
    )))
    segment_info <- append(segment_info, list(list(
      dataset = dataset_name, type = "Unexplained", r = r,
      angle = pi/2 + pi/12,
      perc = round(variance_data$Unexplained[i] * 100)
    )))
  }
  for (seg in segment_info) {
    p <- p + annotate("text", x = seg$r * cos(seg$angle), y = seg$r * sin(seg$angle),
                      label = paste0(seg$perc, "%"), color = "black",
                      fontface = "bold", size = 3)
  }
  
  p <- p + annotate("text", x = 8, y = 4, label = "Explained Variance", 
                    fontface = "bold", size = 4, hjust = 1)
  p <- p + annotate("text", x = -8, y = 4, label = "Unexplained Variance", 
                    fontface = "bold", size = 4, hjust = 0)
  
  # Build reference points
  reference_points <- list()
  if (!is.null(names_list)) {
    for (i in 1:length(names_list)) {
      dataset <- names_list[i]
      r <- radius_mapping[dataset]
      reference_points <- append(reference_points, list(list(dataset = dataset, side = "right",
                                                             x = r * cos(pi/2 - pi/12),
                                                             y = r * sin(pi/2 - pi/12))))
      reference_points <- append(reference_points, list(list(dataset = dataset, side = "left",
                                                             x = r * cos(pi/2 + pi/12),
                                                             y = r * sin(pi/2 + pi/12))))
    }
  } else {
    reference_points <- list(
      list(dataset = "Species", side = "right", x = 4 * cos(pi/2 - pi/12), y = 4 * sin(pi/2 - pi/12)),
      list(dataset = "Pathways", side = "right", x = 3 * cos(pi/2 - pi/12), y = 3 * sin(pi/2 - pi/12)),
      list(dataset = "MAGs", side = "right", x = 2 * cos(pi/2 - pi/12), y = 2 * sin(pi/2 - pi/12)),
      list(dataset = "Species", side = "left", x = 4 * cos(pi/2 + pi/12), y = 4 * sin(pi/2 + pi/12)),
      list(dataset = "Pathways", side = "left", x = 3 * cos(pi/2 + pi/12), y = 3 * sin(pi/2 + pi/12)),
      list(dataset = "MAGs", side = "left", x = 2 * cos(pi/2 + pi/12), y = 2 * sin(pi/2 + pi/12))
    )
  }
  
  explained_label_colors <- character(nrow(variance_data))
  unexplained_label_colors <- character(nrow(variance_data))
  for (i in 1:nrow(variance_data)) {
    dataset_name <- as.character(variance_data$Dataset[i])
    if (dataset_name %in% names(color_mapping)) {
      explained_label_colors[i] <- color_mapping[dataset_name]
      unexplained_label_colors[i] <- adjustcolor(color_mapping[dataset_name], alpha.f = 0.7)
    } else {
      explained_label_colors[i] <- color_palette[min(i, length(color_palette))]
      unexplained_label_colors[i] <- adjustcolor(color_palette[min(i, length(color_palette))], alpha.f = 0.7)
    }
  }
  
  dataset_order <- if (!is.null(names_list)) names_list else c("Species", "Pathways", "MAGs")
  
  # Right side labels
  for (i in 1:length(dataset_order)) {
    dataset <- dataset_order[i]
    dataset_index <- which(as.character(variance_data$Dataset) == dataset)
    if (length(dataset_index) > 0) {
      ref_idx <- which(sapply(reference_points, function(x) x$dataset == dataset && x$side == "right"))
      if (length(ref_idx) > 0) {
        ref_point <- reference_points[[ref_idx[1]]]
        p <- p + annotate("text", x = 8.5, y = 2.5 - (i-1) * 0.7,
                          label = dataset, color = explained_label_colors[dataset_index],
                          fontface = "bold", size = 3.5, hjust = 1)
        p <- p + annotate("segment", x = ref_point$x, y = ref_point$y,
                          xend = 7.5, yend = 2.5 - (i-1) * 0.7,
                          color = "black", size = 0.2)
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Left side labels
  for (i in 1:length(dataset_order)) {
    dataset <- dataset_order[i]
    dataset_index <- which(as.character(variance_data$Dataset) == dataset)
    if (length(dataset_index) > 0) {
      ref_idx <- which(sapply(reference_points, function(x) x$dataset == dataset && x$side == "left"))
      if (length(ref_idx) > 0) {
        ref_point <- reference_points[[ref_idx[1]]]
        p <- p + annotate("text", x = -8.5, y = 2.5 - (i-1) * 0.7,
                          label = dataset, color = unexplained_label_colors[dataset_index],
                          fontface = "bold", size = 3.5, hjust = 0)
        p <- p + annotate("segment", x = ref_point$x, y = ref_point$y,
                          xend = -7.5, yend = 2.5 - (i-1) * 0.7,
                          color = "black", size = 0.2)
      }
    }
  }
  
  p <- p + xlim(-10, 10) + ylim(-5, 5) +
    theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 14, face = "bold"),
          legend.position = "none")
  
  return(p)
}

# Main function for running custom PERMANOVA models with proper sequence
run_custom_permanova_plots <- function(ps_list, names_list, formula_list, 
                                     title_prefix = "PERMANOVA Results",
                                     color_palette = NULL,
                                     tax_glom_list = NULL,
                                     prevalence = 0.1,
                                     detection = 0.01,
                                     use_adjusted_r2 = TRUE) {
  
  require(phyloseq)
  require(microbiome)
  require(vegan)
  require(ggplot2)
  require(dplyr)
  require(tidyr)
  require(forcats)
  require(gridExtra)
  require(patchwork)
  
  if (!is.list(ps_list)) stop("ps_list must be a list of phyloseq objects")
  if (length(ps_list) != length(names_list)) stop("ps_list and names_list must have the same length")
  if (length(ps_list) != length(formula_list)) stop("ps_list and formula_list must have the same length")
  
  if (is.null(tax_glom_list)) tax_glom_list <- rep(NA, length(ps_list))
  if (is.null(color_palette)) {
    color_palette <- c("#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73", "#F0E442", "#0072B2", "#D55E00", "#CC79A7", "#999999")
    if (length(ps_list) > length(color_palette)) {
      color_palette <- colorRampPalette(color_palette)(length(ps_list))
    }
  }
  
  named_colors <- setNames(color_palette[1:length(names_list)], names_list)
  
  bray_results <- list(all_results = list(), combined_data = data.frame())
  bray_variance <- data.frame(Dataset = character(), Explained = numeric(), Unexplained = numeric(), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  
  # Preprocess all datasets
  preprocessed_ps_list <- list()
  for (i in 1:length(ps_list)) {
    ps_obj <- ps_list[[i]]
    name <- names_list[i]
    tax_glom_rank <- tax_glom_list[[i]]
    preprocessed_ps <- preprocess_phyloseq(
      ps_obj = ps_obj, name = name, tax_glom_rank = tax_glom_rank,
      prevalence = prevalence, detection = detection
    )
    preprocessed_ps_list[[i]] <- preprocessed_ps
  }
  
  # Run PERMANOVA on preprocessed data
  for (i in 1:length(preprocessed_ps_list)) {
    ps_obj <- preprocessed_ps_list[[i]]
    name <- names_list[i]
    formula <- formula_list[[i]]
    
    tryCatch({
      bray_result <- run_single_permanova(ps_obj = ps_obj, name = name, formula = formula, method = "bray")
      
      if (!is.null(bray_result$result$permanova_result) && nrow(bray_result$data) > 0) {
        bray_results$all_results[[name]] <- bray_result$result
        bray_results$combined_data <- rbind(bray_results$combined_data, bray_result$data)
        
        residuals_row <- bray_result$data %>% 
          filter(grepl("^Residual", meta, ignore.case = TRUE) | meta == "other")
        
        if (use_adjusted_r2 && "adjR2" %in% colnames(bray_result$data)) {
          total_explained <- sum(bray_result$data$adjR2[!grepl("^Residual", bray_result$data$meta, ignore.case = TRUE) & 
                                                        bray_result$data$meta != "other"], na.rm = TRUE)
          explained_variance <- total_explained
          message(paste("Using ADJUSTED R² for variance calculation in", name))
        } else {
          explained_variance <- if(nrow(residuals_row) > 0) {
            1 - residuals_row$R2[1]
          } else {
            message(paste("Warning: No residual row found for", name, "with Bray-Curtis method"))
            0
          }
        }
        
        unexplained_variance <- 1 - explained_variance
        bray_variance <- rbind(bray_variance, data.frame(
          Dataset = name,
          Explained = explained_variance,
          Unexplained = unexplained_variance,
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        ))
      } else {
        message(paste("No valid results for", name, "- skipping"))
      }
    }, error = function(e) {
      message(paste("Error in Bray-Curtis analysis for", name, ":", e$message))
    })
  }
  
  bray_plot <- NULL
  bray_combined_plot <- NULL
  if (nrow(bray_results$combined_data) > 0) {
    bray_plot_data <- bray_results$combined_data %>% 
      filter(!grepl("^Residual", meta, ignore.case = TRUE) & meta != "other")
    bray_plot_data$Dataset <- factor(bray_plot_data$Dataset, levels = names_list)
    bray_plot_data <- process_combined_data(bray_plot_data, use_adjusted_r2 = use_adjusted_r2)
    
    plot_title <- if (use_adjusted_r2) {
      paste0(title_prefix, ": Bray-Curtis (Adjusted R²)")
    } else {
      paste0(title_prefix, ": Bray-Curtis (R²)")
    }
    
    bray_plot <- create_permanova_plot(
      data = bray_plot_data, title = plot_title,
      color_palette = named_colors, use_adjusted_r2 = use_adjusted_r2
    )
    
    if (nrow(bray_variance) > 0) {
      bray_variance$Dataset <- factor(bray_variance$Dataset, levels = names_list)
      bray_variance <- bray_variance[order(bray_variance$Dataset), ]
      
      donut_title <- if (use_adjusted_r2) {
        "Adjusted Variance Explained (Bray-Curtis)"
      } else {
        "Variance Explained (Bray-Curtis)"
      }
      
      bray_donut_plot <- create_final_improved_donut_plot(
        variance_data = bray_variance, title = donut_title,
        color_palette = color_palette, names_list = names_list,
        use_adjusted_r2 = use_adjusted_r2
      )
      
      bray_combined_plot <- bray_plot + bray_donut_plot + plot_layout(ncol = 2, widths = c(2, 2))
    }
  } else {
    message("No Bray-Curtis results available for plotting")
  }
  
  combined_donut_plot <- NULL
  if (nrow(bray_variance) > 0) {
    donut_title <- if (use_adjusted_r2) {
      "Adjusted Variance Explained (Bray-Curtis)"
    } else {
      "Variance Explained (Bray-Curtis)"
    }
    combined_donut_plot <- create_final_improved_donut_plot(
      variance_data = bray_variance, title = donut_title,
      color_palette = color_palette, names_list = names_list,
      use_adjusted_r2 = use_adjusted_r2
    )
  }
  
  return(list(
    bray_results = bray_results,
    bray_plot = bray_plot,
    bray_combined_plot = bray_combined_plot,
    combined_donut_plot = combined_donut_plot,
    bray_variance = bray_variance,
    preprocessed_ps_list = preprocessed_ps_list,
    used_adjusted_r2 = use_adjusted_r2
  ))
}
```

# permanova r2 adj RUN
```{r}
# Load your phyloseq objects with updated paths
hm_phyloseq_joint <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/hm3/RSF_ps_humann_joint.rds')
mags_ps_filtered <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_ps_mags.rds')
ps_wgs <- readRDS('/home/sonec_iv/RSF_res_4_ps/RSF_all_physeq_wgs_COUNTS_v2.rds')

ps_list <- list(ps_wgs, hm_phyloseq_joint, mags_ps_filtered)
#ps_list <- list(ps_wgs)
names_list <- c("Species", "Pathways", "MAGs")
#names_list <- c("Species")

# DEFINE YOUR SPECIFIC FORMULAS FOR EACH DATASET
formula_pathways <- 'Sex + Hospital + Department + Current_Smoking + Antibiotics_Last_Year + Anticoagulants + Beta_blockers + Corticosteroids + Antineoplastic_Drugs + Antilipidemic_Drugs + NSAIDs + Antiarrhythmic_Drugs + Antibiotics_used + Age_group + Timepoint'
#formula_pathways <- 'Sex + Hospital + Department + Current_Smoking + Antibiotics_Last_Year + Antihypertensive_Drugs + Anticoagulants + Beta_blockers + Proton_Pump_Inhibitors + Corticosteroids + Antineoplastic_Drugs + Antidepressants + Antilipidemic_Drugs + Hypoglycemic_Drugs + Asthma_Treatment_Drugs + NSAIDs + Antiarrhythmic_Drugs + Antibiotics_used + Age_group + Group + Timepoint'
#formula_pathways <- '(Sex + Hospital + Department + Current_Smoking + Antibiotics_Last_Year + Antihypertensive_Drugs + Anticoagulants + Beta_blockers + Proton_Pump_Inhibitors + Corticosteroids + Antineoplastic_Drugs + Antidepressants + Antilipidemic_Drugs + Hypoglycemic_Drugs + Asthma_Treatment_Drugs + NSAIDs + Antiarrhythmic_Drugs + Antibiotics_used + Age_group + Group )*Timepoint'

formula_Species <- 'Sex + Hospital + Current_Smoking + Antibiotics_Last_Year + Beta_blockers + Corticosteroids + Antibiotics_used + Age_group + BMI_group + Timepoint'

#formula_Species <- '(Sex + Hospital + Current_Smoking + Antibiotics_Last_Year + Beta_blockers + Proton_Pump_Inhibitors + Corticosteroids + Antidepressants+ Antibiotics_used + Age_group + BMI_group +Group)*Timepoint'

formula_mags <- 'Sex + Hospital + Department + Current_Smoking + Antibiotics_Last_Year + Anticoagulants + Beta_blockers + Antineoplastic_Drugs + NSAIDs + Antiarrhythmic_Drugs + Antihistamines + Antibiotics_used + Age_group + BMI_group + Timepoint'
#formula_mags <- '(Sex + Hospital + Department + Current_Smoking + Antibiotics_Last_Year + Antihypertensive_Drugs + Anticoagulants + Beta_blockers + Proton_Pump_Inhibitors + Antineoplastic_Drugs + Hypoglycemic_Drugs + Asthma_Treatment_Drugs + NSAIDs + Antiarrhythmic_Drugs + Antihistamines + Antibiotics_used + Age_group + BMI_group + Group)*Timepoint'

formula_list <- list(formula_Species, formula_pathways, formula_mags)

#formula_list <- list(formula_Species)
# FIXED: DEFINE TAXONOMIC AGGLOMERATION FOR EACH DATASET
# WGS: agglomerate by Species
# Pathways: no agglomeration (set to NA)
# MAGs: no agglomeration (set to NA)
tax_glom_list <- list("Species", NA, "Species")

#tax_glom_list <- list("Species")
# MODIFIED: Run the function with ADJUSTED R2 values
custom_results <- run_custom_permanova_plots(
  ps_list = ps_list,
  names_list = names_list,
  formula_list = formula_list,
  title_prefix = "Impact on Microbial Community",
  color_palette = c("#009E73", "#E69F00", "#56B4E9"),  # Green, Orange, Blue
  tax_glom_list = tax_glom_list,
  prevalence = 10/100,    # 10% prevalence
  detection = 1/100,      # 1% detection
  use_adjusted_r2 = TRUE  # SET TO TRUE TO USE ADJUSTED R2 VALUES
)

# Display different plot combinations:

# Option 1: Show only donut plot (now with adjusted R2)
print(custom_results$combined_donut_plot)

# Option 2: Show bar chart + donut plot combined (now with adjusted R2)
bray_combined_plot <- custom_results$bray_combined_plot
print(bray_combined_plot)

# Option 3: Show only bar chart (now with adjusted R2)
print(custom_results$bray_plot)

# Save plots in both PNG and PDF formats
# Combined plot (bar + donut) - NOW WITH ADJUSTED R2
ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_bray_custom_adjustedR2.png', dpi=300, width=30, height=18, bray_combined_plot)
ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_bray_custom_adjustedR2.pdf', width=30, height=18, bray_combined_plot)

# Individual bar plot - NOW WITH ADJUSTED R2
ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_bar_plot_adjustedR2.png', dpi=300, width=30, height=18, custom_results$bray_plot)
ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_bar_plot_adjustedR2.pdf', width=30, height=18, custom_results$bray_plot)

# Individual donut plot - NOW WITH ADJUSTED R2
ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_donut_plot_adjustedR2.png', dpi=300, width=30, height=18, custom_results$combined_donut_plot)
ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_donut_plot_adjustedR2.pdf', width=30, height=18, custom_results$combined_donut_plot)

# Save individual component plots as well
if (!is.null(custom_results$bray_plot)) {
  ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_bar_only_adjustedR2.png', dpi=300, width=30, height=18, custom_results$bray_plot)
  ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_bar_only_adjustedR2.pdf', width=30, height=18, custom_results$bray_plot)
}

if (!is.null(custom_results$combined_donut_plot)) {
  ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_donut_only_adjustedR2.png', dpi=300, width=30, height=18, custom_results$combined_donut_plot)
  ggsave('/home/sonec_iv/permanova_donut_only_adjustedR2.pdf', width=30, height=18, custom_results$combined_donut_plot)
}

# Print confirmation message
message("Plots with ADJUSTED R² saved successfully!")
message("PNG files saved with 300 DPI for high resolution")
message("PDF files saved for publication quality vector graphics")

# You can also access the raw results if needed
print(custom_results$bray_results)
print(custom_results$bray_variance)

# Print which R2 type was used
message(paste("Used adjusted R²:", custom_results$used_adjusted_r2))
```
